channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Brignone beats Goggia for Italian 1-2 in World Cup downhill. Vonn misses gate and does not finish
item.description: GARMISCH-PARTENKIRCHEN, Germany (AP) — Federica Brignone edged out teammate Sofia Goggia by the smallest possible margin for an Italian 1-2 finish in a women’s World Cup downhill on Saturday, while Lindsey Vonn missed a gate near the bottom of the course and failed to finish.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: After three-quarter heavyweight bout, Palo Alto throws knockout punch to beat Los Gatos in league clash
item.description: Prep boys basketball: In a game that resembled a street fight in its physicality, Palo Alto and star guard Jorell Clark threw the last, best punch in the fourth quarter to beat Los Gatos
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Fruita Wildcats dominate both ends of the court in rivalry showdown
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— The Fruita Monument High School Wildcats girls’ basketball team continued their dominant form Tuesday night, following a blowout win over Central with a convincing victory over the Palisade Bulldogs.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Amber Glenn defends her title at the US Figure Skating Championships as Alysa Liu falters late
item.description: WICHITA, Kan. (AP) — Amber Glenn was disappointed but not defeated after her short program at the U.S. Figure Skating Championships, the reigning champion defiantly vowing to “go on the attack” when she returned to the ice for the free skate on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kucherov scores in OT to give Lightning 4-3 win over Blackhawks
item.description: Nikita Kucherov scored 58 seconds into overtime and had two assists as the Tampa Bay Lightning beat the Chicago Blackhawks 4-3 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The 49ers bring back Robert Saleh for 2nd stint as defensive coordinator
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — The San Francisco 49ers hired Robert Saleh for a second stint as defensive coordinator on Friday night after a failed tenure as head coach of the New York Jets. Saleh had been a candidate for head coaching jobs with Jacksonville, Dallas and Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricanes acquire Rantanen from Avs, Hall from Blackhawks in 3-team trade
item.description: The Carolina Hurricanes have acquired forwards Mikko Rantanen from the Colorado Avalanche and Taylor Hall from the Chicago Blackhawks in a three-team trade.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No. 2 South Carolina gets another big win against No. 5 LSU to continue SEC domination
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina showed once more who’s in charge of the Southeastern Conference. The No. 2 Gamecocks (19-1, 7-0 SEC) got 14 points from Joyce Edwards and used their trademark lockdown defense to end No. 5 LSU’s perfect start to the season with a 66-56 victory on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris English makes a late charge to the take 3rd-round lead at the Farmers Insurance Open
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Harris English has been on a roll at Torrey Pines before, so the feeling was familiar when he really got it going late in the third round of the Farmers Insurance Open.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tanner Scott's $72 million, 4-year contract with Dodgers includes $21 million in deferred money
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Reliever Tanner Scott's $72 million, four-year contract with the Los Angeles Dodgers includes $21 million in deferred salaries. Scott receives a $20 million signing bonus as part of the agreement announced Thursday, payable in four equal installments each Feb. 1 from 2025-28.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jalen Hurts practices on injured left knee for Eagles, ready for NFC championship game
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts was a full participant in practice with an injured left knee for the second straight day Friday and is set to start in the NFC championship game.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Riviera tournament moving to Torrey Pines in San Diego because of LA wildfires
item.description: The PGA Tour is relocating the Genesis Invitational out of wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles, announcing Friday it will be held Feb. 13-16 on the South course at Torrey Pines in San Diego with an emphasis on relief initiatives for victims of the fires.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tiger Woods' golf tournament moves from L.A. to San Diego due to fires
item.description: Tiger Woods' PGA golf tournament is moving from the Los Angeles area to San Diego as a result of the fires ravaging Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Patrick Mahomes' grandfather in hospice just days before Chiefs' AFC title game vs. Bills, his mother says
item.description: Patrick Mahomes' mother, Randi Mahomes, announced on Instagram Friday that the quarterback's grandfather is in hospice, amid a turbulent year for the family.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mailbag: How ‘poaching penalty’ lawsuit could impact Pac-12 expansion, travel for Olympic sports, Gonzaga’s rev-share edge(?) and more
item.description: The Pac-12 needs one more football-playing school. The outcome of a lawsuit against the Mountain West could frame the next expansion step.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Djokovic quits mid-match and walks off to boos, putting Zverev in Australian Open final vs. Sinner
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Spectators booed an injured Novak Djokovic as he left the court in Rod Laver Arena after quitting one set into his Australian Open semifinal against Alexander Zverev on Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USF Men's Tennis starts season off undefeated
item.description: The USF Bulls improved to 3-0 with victory at Stetson. The USF Men’s Tennis team earned a 5-2 win in their first road match of the spring season on Monday, Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gilgeous-Alexander torches Jazz for career-high 54
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In a matchup between the Western Conference's best and worst teams, the Utah Jazz (10-32) gave the Oklahoma City Thunder (36-7) quite a scare on their home court.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Runnin' Utes crushed by #7 Houston, 70-36
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - Riding a three-game winning streak, the Runnin' Utes had gained some confidence the last couple of weeks. But Wednesday night against #7 Houston, the Utes came crashing back down to Earth. Milos Uzan scored 14 points, L.J. Cryer added 13 points and No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Mitchell High School alum playing for Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rays are gearing up for the season. Mitchell High School alum and Tampa Bay native, Trevor Harrison, is headed to Rays camp in just a few weeks.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bucs OC Liam Coen withdraws from consideration for Jaguars' coaching job, AP source says
item.description: Tampa Bay offensive coordinator Liam Coen is no longer a candidate to be Jacksonville's next head coach.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG and ROB MAADDI AP Sports Writers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ichiro Suzuki, CC Sabathia and Billy Wagner elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Used to leading off, Ichiro Suzuki got antsy when he had to wait. Considered a no-doubt pick for baseball's Hall of Fame and possibly the second unanimous selection, he waited by the phone for the expected call Tuesday. Fifteen minutes passed without a ring.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: PGA Tour looking to move 2025 Genesis Invitational to Torrey Pines
item.description: The tournament was scheduled to take place from Feb. 13 to 16 at The Riviera Country Club, which is in the Pacific Palisades neighborhood of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Rondé Barber helps build Habitat for Humanity home
item.description: ST. PETERSBURGH, Fla. (WFLA) - Community leaders got together Tuesday to build a home for a family in St. Petersburg. This will serve as the first official build day for Habitat for Humanity Tampa Bay Gulfside's fourth annual CEO Build.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chilean nationals arrested, linked to Joe Burrow home burglary
item.description: An SUV search reportedly found "an old LSU shirt and Bengals hat believed to have been stolen" from the incident.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: CMU Softball ready to chase another dominant season in 2025
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— After a record-breaking 2024 campaign that ended in postseason disappointment, the Colorado Mesa University softball team is gearing up for another dominant season.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Barkley runs for 78- and 62-yard TDs in the snow and Eagles top Rams 28-22 to head to NFC title game
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Saquon Barkley blew through a hole and ran untouched in the wintry mix, the Eagles' 2,000-yard rusher smacking his helmet with his hand on his final snowy steps of a 78-yard touchdown run.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former MLB catcher, AL manager of the year for the White Sox dies at 83
item.description: Jeff Torborg, a former MLB catcher and manager of the Chicago White Sox, died this morning in Westfield, New Jersey, after a career that included a World Series championship and a Manager of the Year award.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
